mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with above-trend GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5 percent signals labor market normalization, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, amplifying aggregate demand shocks that translate into elevated equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and signal central bank tightening, which mechanically suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS rises consecutively for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, reducing input cost inflation and supporting corporate margins, thereby lifting short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference between high and low INDPRO growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding a monthly increase of 200,000 demonstrate robust labor demand, which fuels wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of months where PAYEMS increases exceed 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total employment and declining unemployment rates confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, reducing uncertainty premiums and supporting sustained equity appreciation over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 40 percent of 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 3.0 percent annualized trigger inflation hedging flows and central bank hawkishness, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent annualized.
